learning_outcomes.description: Identify principles, methods and frameworks for integrating design into business.
Identify opportunities for design-driven innovation and organisational change through critical examination of business models and strategies.
Develop innovative and commercially viable product and service solutions using design principles, methods, and framework.
Pitch design proposals persuasively to professional audiences, demonstrating leadership in presenting design outcomes that align with strategic business objectives.
Collaborate in interdisciplinary teams to plan, manage and present well-reasoned design solutions that respond to real-world business challenges.
Recognise the social, environmental, and commercial impact of design decisions on business and organisational settings.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 1 - Individual Business Design Analysis Poster
assessments.due_date:
Digital submission 16/03/2026 4:00 pm

Presentation of the Printed Poster in class 17/03/2026
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

In this individual assessment, students will produce an A3 poster, which will be printed and presented physically in class, accompanied by a short verbal explanation. The assessment is designed to develop students’ ability to analyse organisations through a design-led lens and to communicate complex insights clearly and concisely.

Students will conduct in-depth primary and secondary research into a selected organisation (the name of the organisation will be provided). The research findings should be synthesised and presented visually, with a strong focus on clarity, coherence, and strategic insight.

The poster should include the following elements:

  • Organisation name and logo
  • Clear understanding of the organisation’s mission statement, strategy, and value proposition
  • Analysis of the organisation’s business model, including key elements and sources of competitive advantage
  • Assessment of how the business model has evolved or been sustained over time
  • Evaluation of what makes the organisation innovative within its sector or market

Students are required to investigate and present how the organisation has used and integrated design across its products, services, and strategic decision-making. Information should not be reproduced directly from online sources. Instead, students must demonstrate understanding and critical thinking by synthesising information into a clear, accessible, and well-structured visual narrative.

This assessment emphasises:

  • Strategic thinking through design
  • Research synthesis and interpretation
  • Visual communication and clarity
  • Application of theory to real-world organisations

Assessment tasks prohibiting use of AI or MT: This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission. To meet professional accreditation, public engagement and quality assurance obligations, digital copies of all course assessment items must be submitted in addition to any hard copy submission requirements specified in individual Course / Studio Outlines. Any physical models should be photographed and a minimum of two photographs describing the complete and full model must be included in the digital submission.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 2 - Design Freeze and Concept Presentation
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

In their respective inter-disciplinary teams, which will be assigned during the studio in week 1, the students are to identify a previous design idea which they have already developed, create a new design idea, or synthesise existing and new ideas to define an improved product or service. The product or service must be suitable for development and release as a commercial solution, within an 18-24 month timeframe. To assist with this, students will be introduced to key drivers of design innovation, across the lectures and studios in weeks 1-6 including methods for identifying organisational and personal strengths. Each team will present their ideas, market gap, value assessment, and approach to design. Each team member must contribute their own design concept for team consideration.

The students should present their teams design concepts, as well as the strengths of the team in relation to that product or service. This assessment consists of four parts: (a) all individual design concepts presented (problem/solution definition), (b) reflections, (c) group presentation, and (d) team roles/responsibilities. All parts are to be submitted digitally in .PDF format in one consolidated group concept project book.

Note: each group assessment requires individual completion of a peer review exercise (via Blackboard) in which the students rate the performance of their team members. Completion of the peer review is mandatory.

If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.

In-person assessment prohibits use of AI and/or MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 3 - Final Design Presentation & Final Pitch
assessments.due_date:
Digital submission 25/05/2026 4:00 pm

Pitches with prototypes, printed posters and handout materials - in class 26/05/2026
assessments.category: Presentation, Portfolio, Poster, Product/ Design, Project
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

This final team task involves a presentation of the team's final design and business case that contains final visualisations of the teams' identified solution, evidence of a final prototype(s), and fully resolved details for a convincing business case. The purpose of the business case is to capture the reasoning for initiating a product and business, and ultimately to sell the project to financial investors. For this final team assessment, the teams should produce a final design visualisation (included in the printed presentation poster and in the concept book in .PDF format), a final design prototype (presented as a tangible presentation artifact or as a digital platform/app on a device), and a business case for the launch of your new venture (submitted digitally in .PDF format, key aspects included in the pitch). 

Prototype(s) component: the students are free to choose any appropriate techniques. These might include creating scale models, physical mock-ups, digital interfaces, simulations, etc. You are able to use the fabrication facilities in our school if required, as long as you have completed the requisite inductions. Document the process visually, and bring any physical prototypes to the final pitch.

Design concept book (submitted digitally as one .PDF document): note the teams' chosen techniques, justifications, and reflections on any challenges you encountered. All parts of the project workings and concept development should be documented collated to show the team amount of work effort in this project.

Business Case (submitted digitally as one .PDF document): Describe, document, and justify your final decisions regarding the organisation’s competitive advantage(s), target market size or market share, intellectual property management, path to market, market entry strategy, and ongoing business model. This should include a clear articulation of user requirements, evidence of product or service testing, and insights derived from market feedback.

Note: each group assessment requires individual completion of a peer review exercise (via Blackboard) in which the students rate the performance of your team members. Completion of the peer review is mandatory.

If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.

In-person assessment prohibits use of AI and/or MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
